summary:
The bug ("needle") is in `src/config.rs:35-36`, where segment assignments overlap by 2 segments, causing concurrent writes to the same ranges without synchronization, leading to non-deterministic data corruption at boundaries.

Fixed by removing the overlap (`end = std::cmp::min(start + segments_per_worker, self.num_segments);`).

Ran `./test.sh`: All tests now pass with no overlaps or corruption detected.
